findings.title: Literal placeholder ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN="ollama" (not a real secret)
findings.description: When the AI backend is Ollama, the code injects ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN="ollama" and an empty ANTHROPIC_API_KEY so the `claude` CLI talks to a local Ollama-served OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible endpoint. The string "ollama" is a well-known required-but-ignored placeholder for Ollama's auth, not a credential. No real token is present, and the API key is deliberately blanked. This is the documented design (see CLAUDE.md). Listed only so a reviewer does not mistake the `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` assignment for a leaked token.
findings.remediation: No action required. This is a safe placeholder, not a secret. Real Anthropic credentials are never hardcoded — they come from the user's own `claude` CLI login / environment at runtime.

findings.title: CI uses standard GitHub-provided ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} (not a leaked secret)
findings.context:
uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
findings.description: The release workflow passes the automatic, ephemeral GITHUB_TOKEN that GitHub Actions injects per-run (used here by tauri-action to create the draft release). This is a reference to a managed secret, not a hardcoded value, and is the standard, recommended pattern. No secret value is embedded anywhere in the workflow. No other secrets are echoed, printed, or written to logs/artifacts in this file.
findings.remediation: No action required. Optionally scope `permissions:` to the minimum (already limited to `contents: write`), which is correct for release creation.
